text.title: Agentic Positioning Workflow
text.summary: Orchestrator pattern, value-laddering fan-out, devil's-advocate pass, and critical gotchas for LLM-driven competitive positioning.

rule.statement: Run Dunford's six steps serially because each step constrains the next. Fan out only the value-translation step (one parallel task per attribute) since attributes are independent.
rule.rationale: Steps 1-4 have hard dependencies: alternatives must exist before attributes, attributes before value, value before best-fit customer. Parallelizing constrained steps produces incoherent outputs.

rule.statement: Run positioning AFTER competitor-analysis AND user-personas exist. Both are inputs; canvas without them is fiction.
rule.rationale: Hallucinated competitor positioning (agent invents taglines from training data rather than current homepages) is the dominant quality failure. Real competitor pages must be fetched and passed as input.

rule.statement: Always run a devil's-advocate pass with a different model (sonnet → opus) that role-plays each top-3 competitor's CMO trying to dismiss your positioning. Iterate once based on the critique.
rule.rationale: Self-serving positioning is invisible to the agent that produced it. An adversarial pass from the competitor's perspective surfaces weaknesses that peer review misses.

example.code: Build positioning canvas for {product}. Inputs: - .aidocs/product_docs/competitive-analysis.md (alternatives + matrix) - .aidocs/product_docs/user-personas.md (best-fit-customer source) Use Dunford's 6-step framework. Hard rules: - No comparatives ("better/faster/easier") without dimension + number. - Every unique attribute must have a time-to-copy estimate in quarters. - Reject any best-fit segment that includes >30% of alternatives' user bases. Output: positioning-canvas.md + positioning-statement.md (one page). Then spawn devil's-advocate Task from each top-3 competitor's CMO POV; iterate once.

So-what ladder for attribute: '{attribute}'. Three levels: feature benefit -> business benefit -> emotional/strategic outcome. Bind the outcome to a measurable: hours saved, revenue, retention, risk reduction. No vague verbs ("improve", "enhance"). Each level must use a different noun class.

antipattern.description: Comparative language by default: LLMs produce "better/faster/easier" even when prompted for differentiation.
antipattern.reason: Explicit prompt rule plus positioning-lint.sh pre-commit hook catches it. Comparative without dimension+number is the single most common positioning quality failure from LLM agents.

antipattern.description: Hallucinated competitor positioning from training data instead of current homepages.
antipattern.reason: Pass live homepage HTML/text into the prompt. Never let the agent rely on memory for competitor claims — they are updated constantly and the agent's training is stale.

antipattern.description: Ladder collapse: so-what laddering produces three levels that paraphrase the same idea.
antipattern.reason: Force each level to use a different noun class (feature benefit → business outcome → emotional/strategic outcome) and a measurable at the terminal level.

antipattern.description: Category-creation inflation: every product becomes "the first AI-native X."
antipattern.reason: Require a citation that the proposed category does not yet exist — G2 search returning zero results, or no Wikipedia entry for the category name. Fiction categories harm credibility.

antipattern.description: Proof points filled with "trusted by leaders" marketing copy.
antipattern.reason: Every proof point must be a citable URL, customer quote, or measurable outcome. Marketing copy is not evidence and fails the agent's own lint rules.

list.item: Approve the unique-attributes shortlist (no more than 3) before value-laddering begins
Sign off on best-fit-customer narrowing before the statement is drafted
Approve the final positioning statement before it enters marketing, landing-page, or pricing copy — this is a strategic decision agents must not own

text.reference: Lint comparative language and verify six-clause structure
Canvas template with all required sections
